note: Deep CORAL: aligns 2nd-order feature statistics (covariance) between source/target domains via a linear whiten-recolor transform, unsupervised (no target labels needed). Motivated a fresh feature-space alignment lever distinct from every prior shift-mitigation tried (RMS norm, importance-weighting, model-family swap, augmentation) -- queued as exp_048.

Regularized self-training/pseudo-labeling for tabular data under distribution shift -- density-weighted pseudo-label acceptance to avoid drifting toward majority classes. Considered for this competition's train/test shift but higher-risk (label leakage into eval, needs careful fold-honest implementation, and 82-class/rare-class structure makes pseudo-label noise compounding a real risk) -- lower priority than exp_048 (CORAL), queued as a follow-up only if CORAL is positive.

Power Series Template (PoST) model for missing-fundamental pitch perception -- corroborates exp_005's harmonic-template-matching design (grid of candidate f0s scored by harmonic evidence) as neuroscience-aligned, but no new actionable lever; same convergence pattern as block 3's Frontiers paper.

Multimodal co-training under distribution shift: dual-threshold pseudo-labeling + cross-view agreement selects higher-quality pseudo-labels than single-model confidence threshold under train/test shift. Motivates exp_061: gate pseudo-labels on XGBoost+LGBM prediction AGREEMENT (not just XGBoost's own confidence) before adding real test rows to the training set -- distinct mechanism from all 3 prior pseudo-labeling axes tried (threshold sweep exp_051/056/058/059, iterative multi-round exp_055 closed negative, EM prior-correction exp_060 closed negative) and distinct from blend-of-final-predictions (closed 2/2 negative) since agreement is used only to filter WHICH rows get pseudo-labeled, not to combine final predictions.

FMAPLS: Dirichlet-regularized dynamic label-shift prior estimation, ~40% lower KL vs unregularized baselines under severe imbalance -- candidate fix for exp_059's catastrophic EM-prior failure (small per-class test counts made raw EM noisy/harmful)

RLLS (Azizzadenesheli et al., regularized learning of label shift) -- prior art establishing that unregularized MLE/EM label-shift correction is unstable under small samples and needs explicit regularization; supports exp_062's shrinkage design even though not itself fetched in full
CMVN channel-mismatch normalization -- motivated exp_064 row-wise feature normalization probe
utterance-specific mean/variance normalization -- same motivation as exp_064

Gradient Boosting Trees and LLMs for Tabular Data Few-Shot Learning -- min_data_in_leaf=20->1 (plus num_leaves=4, extra_trees=True, feature_fraction=0.5, bagging_fraction=0.5) fixes LightGBM stalling on few-shot-per-class tabular data (default min_data_in_leaf blocks splitting when a class has <20 samples), 290% reported improvement in matching regime.

Snell et al. 2017 Prototypical Networks for Few-Shot Learning -- nearest-class-mean classification in a learned/projected embedding space, standard baseline for exactly the <10-samples/class regime this competition has for 14/82 classes.

Episodic fine-tuning prototypical networks for audio classification -- audio-domain confirmation that prototypical/metric-learning approaches are used for few-shot audio classification specifically; PDF fetch failed to extract readable text, treating as a corroborating pointer only, not a detailed mechanism source.
